Clinical and Administrative Processes.label: Value-Based Care
Clinical and Administrative Processes.definition: A healthcare delivery model where providers are paid based on patient health outcomes, not just services delivered.

Clinical and Administrative Processes.label: Care Coordination
Clinical and Administrative Processes.definition: Organizing patient care activities and sharing information among all participants concerned with a patient's care.

Clinical and Administrative Processes.label: Prior Authorization
Clinical and Administrative Processes.definition: A process required by some health insurers before they will cover specific services, medications, or durable medical equipment.

Clinical and Administrative Processes.label: Quality Measurement
Clinical and Administrative Processes.definition: The process of using data to evaluate the performance of health care providers against recognized quality standards.

Clinical and Administrative Processes.label: Information Blocking
Clinical and Administrative Processes.definition: A practice by a health IT developer, HIE, HIN, or provider that is likely to interfere with the access of EHI.

Clinical and Administrative Processes.label: Telehealth
Clinical and Administrative Processes.definition: The use of electronic information and telecommunication technologies to support long-distance clinical health care.

Clinical and Administrative Processes.label: Credentialing
Clinical and Administrative Processes.definition: The process of obtaining, verifying, and assessing the qualifications of a healthcare practitioner.

Clinical and Administrative Processes.label: Patient Matching
Clinical and Administrative Processes.definition: The process of accurately linking a patient's data across different health IT systems and encounters.

Clinical and Administrative Processes.label: Remote Patient Monitoring
Clinical and Administrative Processes.definition: A method of healthcare delivery that uses technology to monitor patient health outside of a traditional clinical setting.

Clinical and Administrative Processes.label: Risk Adjustment
Clinical and Administrative Processes.definition: A statistical process that accounts for underlying health status when comparing outcomes or costs among patient populations.

Clinical and Administrative Processes.label: Patient-Centered Care
Clinical and Administrative Processes.definition: Providing care that is respectful of and responsive to individual patient preferences, needs, and values.

Clinical and Administrative Processes.label: Claims Processing
Clinical and Administrative Processes.definition: The system used by insurance companies to manage requests for payment for healthcare services.

Clinical and Administrative Processes.label: Digital Therapeutics
Clinical and Administrative Processes.definition: Evidence-based therapeutic interventions driven by software to prevent, manage, or treat a medical disorder or disease.

Clinical and Administrative Processes.label: Remote Therapeutic Monitoring
Clinical and Administrative Processes.definition: Monitoring of non-physiological patient data, such as medication adherence, therapy response, and pain levels.

Clinical and Administrative Processes.label: Informed Consent
Clinical and Administrative Processes.definition: The process where a patient receives information and gives permission before medical treatment or participation in research.

Clinical and Administrative Processes.label: e-Prescribing
Clinical and Administrative Processes.definition: The electronic generation and transmission of a prescription order from a provider to a pharmacy.

Data Standards and Vocabularies.label: FHIR
Data Standards and Vocabularies.definition: Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources, a standard for exchanging healthcare information electronically.

Data Standards and Vocabularies.label: USCDI
Data Standards and Vocabularies.definition: The United States Core Data for Interoperability, a standardized set of health data classes and elements for nationwide interoperability.
terms: United States Core Data for Interoperability

Data Standards and Vocabularies.label: HL7
Data Standards and Vocabularies.definition: Health Level Seven, a set of international standards for transfer of clinical and administrative data between software applications.

Data Standards and Vocabularies.label: Bulk FHIR
Data Standards and Vocabularies.definition: A standard for exporting large volumes of FHIR data from a server.

Data Standards and Vocabularies.label: SMART on FHIR
Data Standards and Vocabularies.definition: A set of open specifications to integrate apps with EHRs, portals, HIEs, and other health IT systems.

Data Standards and Vocabularies.label: NPI
Data Standards and Vocabularies.definition: National Provider Identifier, a unique 10-digit identification number issued to health care providers in the United States.

Data Standards and Vocabularies.label: OpenID Connect
Data Standards and Vocabularies.definition: A simple identity layer on top of the OAuth 2.0 protocol, allowing clients to verify the identity of the end-user.

Data Standards and Vocabularies.label: OAuth 2.0
Data Standards and Vocabularies.definition: An open standard for access delegation, commonly used as a way for users to grant websites or applications access to their information.

Data Standards and Vocabularies.label: CPT
Data Standards and Vocabularies.definition: Current Procedural Terminology, a medical code set maintained by the AMA to report medical, surgical, and diagnostic procedures.

Data Standards and Vocabularies.label: SNOMED CT
Data Standards and Vocabularies.definition: Systematized Nomenclature of Medicine - Clinical Terms, a comprehensive, multilingual clinical healthcare terminology.

Data Standards and Vocabularies.label: C-CDA
Data Standards and Vocabularies.definition: Consolidated Clinical Document Architecture, a standard for creating electronic clinical documents.

Data Standards and Vocabularies.label: CDS Hooks
Data Standards and Vocabularies.definition: A standard for invoking external clinical decision support services from within an EHR workflow.

Data Standards and Vocabularies.label: LOINC
Data Standards and Vocabularies.definition: Logical Observation Identifiers Names and Codes, a standard for identifying medical laboratory observations.
terms: Logical Observation Identifiers Names and Codes

Data Standards and Vocabularies.label: ICD-10
Data Standards and Vocabularies.definition: International Classification of Diseases, 10th Revision, a medical classification list for diagnoses.

Data Standards and Vocabularies.label: X12
Data Standards and Vocabularies.definition: A standard for electronic data interchange (EDI) for business transactions, including healthcare claims.

Data Standards and Vocabularies.label: DICOM
Data Standards and Vocabularies.definition: Digital Imaging and Communications in Medicine, the standard for communicating and managing medical imaging information and related data.
terms: Digital Imaging and Communications in Medicine

Data Standards and Vocabularies.label: HCPCS
Data Standards and Vocabularies.definition: Healthcare Common Procedure Coding System, a set of health care procedure codes based on the CPT.

Data Standards and Vocabularies.label: NCPDP
Data Standards and Vocabularies.definition: National Council for Prescription Drug Programs, a standards development organization for the pharmacy services industry.

Data Standards and Vocabularies.label: QRDA
Data Standards and Vocabularies.definition: Quality Reporting Document Architecture, a standard format for communicating health care quality measurement information.

Data Standards and Vocabularies.label: RxNorm
Data Standards and Vocabularies.definition: A standardized nomenclature for clinical drugs and a tool for supporting semantic interoperation between drug terminologies.

Government Agencies and Bodies.label: ONC
Government Agencies and Bodies.definition: The Office of the National Coordinator for Health Information Technology, an entity within HHS to promote a national health IT infrastructure.
terms: Office of the National Coordinator for Health Information Technology

Government Agencies and Bodies.label: ASTP
Government Agencies and Bodies.definition: The Assistant Secretary for Technology Policy, an office within HHS responsible for health technology policy.

Government Agencies and Bodies.label: HHS
Government Agencies and Bodies.definition: The U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, the U.S. government's principal agency for protecting American health.
terms: Department of Health and Human Services
U.S. Department of Health and Human Services

Government Agencies and Bodies.label: NIST
Government Agencies and Bodies.definition: The National Institute of Standards and Technology, a non-regulatory agency of the U.S. Department of Commerce.
terms: National Institute of Standards and Technology

Government Agencies and Bodies.label: FDA
Government Agencies and Bodies.definition: The Food and Drug Administration, a federal agency responsible for protecting public health by regulating drugs, medical devices, and food.

Government Agencies and Bodies.label: CMMI
Government Agencies and Bodies.definition: The Center for Medicare & Medicaid Innovation, a CMS entity that tests new payment and service delivery models.

Government Agencies and Bodies.label: VA
Government Agencies and Bodies.definition: The U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs, providing healthcare services to eligible military veterans.

Government Agencies and Bodies.label: CDC
Government Agencies and Bodies.definition: The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, the national public health agency of the United States.
terms: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

Government Agencies and Bodies.label: OCR
Government Agencies and Bodies.definition: The Office for Civil Rights, an agency within HHS responsible for enforcing HIPAA and other civil rights laws.

Government Agencies and Bodies.label: NIH
Government Agencies and Bodies.definition: The National Institutes of Health, the primary U.S. agency responsible for biomedical and public health research.

Government Agencies and Bodies.label: SSA
Government Agencies and Bodies.definition: The Social Security Administration, an independent agency of the U.S. government that administers Social Security.

Government Agencies and Bodies.label: OIG
Government Agencies and Bodies.definition: The Office of Inspector General for HHS, responsible for combating fraud, waste, and abuse in HHS programs.

Government Agencies and Bodies.label: FTC
Government Agencies and Bodies.definition: The Federal Trade Commission, an independent agency responsible for promoting consumer protection and preventing anticompetitive business practices.

Government Agencies and Bodies.label: HRSA
Government Agencies and Bodies.definition: The Health Resources and Services Administration, an agency of HHS for improving health care to geographically isolated or vulnerable populations.

Government Agencies and Bodies.label: NLM
Government Agencies and Bodies.definition: The National Library of Medicine, the world's largest biomedical library, which maintains and distributes key health data standards.

Government Agencies and Bodies.label: AHRQ
Government Agencies and Bodies.definition: The Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality, a federal agency focused on producing evidence to make healthcare safer and higher quality.
terms: Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality

Government Agencies and Bodies.label: FCC
Government Agencies and Bodies.definition: The Federal Communications Commission, an independent U.S. government agency that regulates interstate and international communications.

Government Agencies and Bodies.label: HHS OIG
Government Agencies and Bodies.definition: The Office of Inspector General for the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services.

Government Agencies and Bodies.label: MedPAC
Government Agencies and Bodies.definition: The Medicare Payment Advisory Commission, a non-partisan agency that advises the U.S. Congress on the Medicare program.

Health Conditions and Concepts.label: Interoperability
Health Conditions and Concepts.definition: The ability of different information technology systems and software applications to communicate, exchange data, and use the information.

Health Conditions and Concepts.label: Provider Burden
Health Conditions and Concepts.definition: The administrative, regulatory, and cognitive workload placed on healthcare providers that can detract from patient care.

Health Conditions and Concepts.label: Chronic Condition
Health Conditions and Concepts.definition: A human health condition or disease that is persistent or otherwise long-lasting in its effects.

Health Conditions and Concepts.label: SDOH
Health Conditions and Concepts.definition: Social Determinants of Health, non-medical factors that influence health outcomes, like economic stability and education.

Health Conditions and Concepts.label: Behavioral Health
Health Conditions and Concepts.definition: The connection between behaviors and the health and well-being of the body, mind, and spirit.

Health Conditions and Concepts.label: Patient Safety
Health Conditions and Concepts.definition: The prevention of errors and adverse effects to patients associated with health care.

Health Conditions and Concepts.label: Mental Health
Health Conditions and Concepts.definition: A person’s condition with regard to their psychological and emotional well-being.

Health Conditions and Concepts.label: Diabetes
Health Conditions and Concepts.definition: A chronic disease that occurs when the pancreas does not produce enough insulin or when the body cannot effectively use it.

Health Conditions and Concepts.label: Cancer
Health Conditions and Concepts.definition: A disease in which some of the body's cells grow uncontrollably and spread to other parts of the body.

Health Conditions and Concepts.label: Health Equity
Health Conditions and Concepts.definition: The state in which everyone has a fair and just opportunity to attain their highest level of health.

Health Conditions and Concepts.label: Health Literacy
Health Conditions and Concepts.definition: The degree to which individuals have the ability to find, understand, and use information to inform health-related decisions.

Health Conditions and Concepts.label: Hypertension
Health Conditions and Concepts.definition: A condition in which the force of the blood against the artery walls is too high; also known as high blood pressure.

Health Conditions and Concepts.label: Dementia
Health Conditions and Concepts.definition: A general term for loss of memory, language, problem-solving and other thinking abilities that are severe enough to interfere with daily life.

Health Conditions and Concepts.label: Heart Failure
Health Conditions and Concepts.definition: A chronic condition in which the heart doesn't pump blood as well as it should.

Health Conditions and Concepts.label: Stroke
Health Conditions and Concepts.definition: A medical condition in which poor blood flow to the brain results in cell death.

Health Conditions and Concepts.label: Substance Use Disorder
Health Conditions and Concepts.definition: A disease that affects a person's brain and behavior and leads to an inability to control the use of a legal or illegal drug.

Health Conditions and Concepts.label: Opioid Use Disorder
Health Conditions and Concepts.definition: A problematic pattern of opioid use that causes significant impairment or distress.

Health Conditions and Concepts.label: Alzheimer's Disease
Health Conditions and Concepts.definition: A brain disorder that slowly destroys memory and thinking skills, and eventually the ability to carry out the simplest tasks.

Health Information Technology and System.label: Electronic Health Record
Health Information Technology and System.definition: A digital version of a patient's paper chart, containing the medical and treatment history of a patient.

Health Information Technology and System.label: API
Health Information Technology and System.definition: An Application Programming Interface, a set of rules allowing different software applications to communicate with each other.

Health Information Technology and System.label: Artificial Intelligence
Health Information Technology and System.definition: The simulation of human intelligence in machines that are programmed to think and learn, used in healthcare for various tasks.

Health Information Technology and System.label: Digital Identity Credential
Health Information Technology and System.definition: A digital representation of an individual's identity used for authentication and access to online services.

Health Information Technology and System.label: Health IT
Health Information Technology and System.definition: Health Information Technology, the application of information processing involving both computer hardware and software that deals with health information.

Health Information Technology and System.label: Provider Directory
Health Information Technology and System.definition: A list of doctors, hospitals, and other healthcare providers that a health plan has contracted with to provide medical care.

Health Information Technology and System.label: Health Info Exchange
Health Information Technology and System.definition: A system that enables the electronic movement of health-related information among organizations according to nationally recognized standards.

Health Information Technology and System.label: QHIN
Health Information Technology and System.definition: A Qualified Health Information Network, an entity designated by ONC to connect to other QHINs for nationwide data exchange.

Health Information Technology and System.label: Patient Portal
Health Information Technology and System.definition: A secure online website that gives patients convenient, 24-hour access to personal health information from anywhere with an internet connection.

Health Information Technology and System.label: CEHRT
Health Information Technology and System.definition: Certified Electronic Health Record Technology that meets standards set by the ONC Health IT Certification Program.

Health Information Technology and System.label: Clinical Decision Support
Health Information Technology and System.definition: Health IT functionality that provides clinicians with knowledge and person-specific information to enhance health and health care.

Health Information Technology and System.label: Large Language Model
Health Information Technology and System.definition: A type of artificial intelligence model trained on vast amounts of text data to understand and generate human-like language.

Health Information Technology and System.label: Machine Learning
Health Information Technology and System.definition: A subset of artificial intelligence that involves training algorithms to learn patterns from data without being explicitly programmed.

Health Information Technology and System.label: Natural Language Processing
Health Information Technology and System.definition: A field of AI that enables computers to understand, interpret, and manipulate human language from text or speech.

Health Information Technology and System.label: eCQM
Health Information Technology and System.definition: Electronic Clinical Quality Measures, which use data from EHRs to track the quality of healthcare services.

Health Information Technology and System.label: dQM
Health Information Technology and System.definition: Digital Quality Measures, an evolution of eCQMs that use digital data sources and standards for quality measurement.

Health Information Technology and System.label: Data Registry
Health Information Technology and System.definition: An organized system that uses observational study methods to collect uniform data to evaluate specified outcomes for a population.

Health Information Technology and System.label: Personal Health Record
Health Information Technology and System.definition: An electronic application through which individuals can maintain and manage their health information in a private, secure environment.

Healthcare Data and Documents.label: Claims Data
Healthcare Data and Documents.definition: Information submitted by providers to insurance companies for payment of services.

Healthcare Data and Documents.label: EHI
Healthcare Data and Documents.definition: Electronic Health Information, which is electronic protected health information (ePHI) in a designated record set.

Healthcare Data and Documents.label: Care Plan
Healthcare Data and Documents.definition: A document that outlines a patient's health goals and the actions required to meet them.

Healthcare Data and Documents.label: Clinical Note
Healthcare Data and Documents.definition: A written record made by a healthcare professional detailing a patient's clinical status or course of treatment.

Healthcare Data and Documents.label: PHI
Healthcare Data and Documents.definition: Protected Health Information, any information in a medical record that can be used to identify an individual.

Healthcare Data and Documents.label: ADT Data
Healthcare Data and Documents.definition: Admission, Discharge, and Transfer data, which tracks patient movements within and between healthcare facilities.

Healthcare Data and Documents.label: Biometric Data
Healthcare Data and Documents.definition: Physical or behavioral human characteristics that can be used to digitally identify a person to grant access to systems.

Healthcare Data and Documents.label: Encounter Data
Healthcare Data and Documents.definition: Data from patient-provider interactions, used particularly in managed care settings like Medicare Advantage.

Healthcare Data and Documents.label: Operative Report
Healthcare Data and Documents.definition: A report written by a surgeon that details a surgical procedure.

Healthcare Data and Documents.label: Patient-Reported Outcome
Healthcare Data and Documents.definition: Health outcomes reported directly by the patient, without interpretation by a clinician.

Healthcare Data and Documents.label: Discharge Summary
Healthcare Data and Documents.definition: A clinical report prepared by a healthcare professional at the conclusion of a hospital stay or series of treatments.

Healthcare Data and Documents.label: EOB
Healthcare Data and Documents.definition: An Explanation of Benefits, a statement sent by a health insurance company explaining what medical treatments were paid for.

Healthcare Data and Documents.label: PII
Healthcare Data and Documents.definition: Personally Identifiable Information, any data that could potentially identify a specific individual.

Healthcare Data and Documents.label: Patient-Generated Data
Healthcare Data and Documents.definition: Health-related data created, recorded, or gathered by or from patients to help address a health concern.

Healthcare Data and Documents.label: Advance Directive
Healthcare Data and Documents.definition: A legal document that specifies what actions should be taken for a person's health if they are no longer able to make decisions.

Healthcare Programs and Initiatives.label: Medicare
Healthcare Programs and Initiatives.definition: A federal health insurance program for people 65 or older and certain younger people with disabilities.

Healthcare Programs and Initiatives.label: Medicaid
Healthcare Programs and Initiatives.definition: A joint federal and state program that provides health coverage to millions of Americans with limited income and resources.

Healthcare Programs and Initiatives.label: TEFCA
Healthcare Programs and Initiatives.definition: The Trusted Exchange Framework and Common Agreement, a national policy and technical framework for health information exchange.
terms: Trusted Exchange Framework and Common Agreement

Healthcare Programs and Initiatives.label: Blue Button
Healthcare Programs and Initiatives.definition: A CMS initiative allowing beneficiaries to securely download their health information from their Medicare account.

Healthcare Programs and Initiatives.label: Medicare Advantage
Healthcare Programs and Initiatives.definition: An 'all in one' alternative to Original Medicare, offered by private companies. Also known as Part C.

Healthcare Programs and Initiatives.label: MIPS
Healthcare Programs and Initiatives.definition: The Merit-based Incentive Payment System, a program that determines Medicare payment adjustments for eligible clinicians.

Healthcare Programs and Initiatives.label: MSSP
Healthcare Programs and Initiatives.definition: The Medicare Shared Savings Program, an Alternative Payment Model that offers providers financial incentives to coordinate care for Medicare beneficiaries.

Healthcare Programs and Initiatives.label: Promoting Interoperability
Healthcare Programs and Initiatives.definition: A CMS program that encourages eligible professionals and hospitals to adopt, implement, upgrade, and demonstrate meaningful use of CEHRT.

Healthcare Programs and Initiatives.label: Data at Point of Care
Healthcare Programs and Initiatives.definition: A CMS pilot program that provides clinicians with access to their patients' Medicare claims data at the point of care.
terms: Data at the Point of Care

Healthcare Programs and Initiatives.label: Medicare Part D
Healthcare Programs and Initiatives.definition: The part of Medicare that provides outpatient prescription drug coverage.

Healthcare Programs and Initiatives.label: Medicare Part A
Healthcare Programs and Initiatives.definition: The part of Medicare that covers inpatient hospital stays, care in a skilled nursing facility, hospice care, and home health care.

Healthcare Programs and Initiatives.label: CHIP
Healthcare Programs and Initiatives.definition: The Children's Health Insurance Program, providing low-cost health coverage to children in families who earn too much to qualify for Medicaid.

Healthcare Programs and Initiatives.label: ACO REACH Model
Healthcare Programs and Initiatives.definition: An Alternative Payment Model testing a new payment approach to better support care for Medicare beneficiaries in ACOs.
terms: Accountable Care Organization Realizing Equity, Access, and Community Health

Healthcare Programs and Initiatives.label: QPP
Healthcare Programs and Initiatives.definition: The Quality Payment Program, which rewards high-value, high-quality Medicare clinicians with payment increases.

Healthcare Programs and Initiatives.label: Medicare DPP
Healthcare Programs and Initiatives.definition: The Medicare Diabetes Prevention Program, a health behavior change program to help prevent type 2 diabetes in at-risk beneficiaries.

Healthcare Programs and Initiatives.label: Medicare Part B
Healthcare Programs and Initiatives.definition: The part of Medicare that covers certain doctors' services, outpatient care, medical supplies, and preventive services.

Healthcare Programs and Initiatives.label: TCET Pathway
Healthcare Programs and Initiatives.definition: The Transitional Coverage for Emerging Technologies pathway to expedite Medicare coverage of new, innovative medical devices.

Laws and Regulations.label: HIPAA
Laws and Regulations.definition: The Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act of 1996, a federal law protecting sensitive patient health information.
terms: Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act
Health Information Portability and Accountability Act

Laws and Regulations.label: Cures Act
Laws and Regulations.definition: The 21st Century Cures Act, a 2016 law designed to accelerate medical product development and bring innovations to patients faster.

Laws and Regulations.label: Information Blocking Rule
Laws and Regulations.definition: A rule implementing provisions of the 21st Century Cures Act to prevent interference with access to electronic health information.

Laws and Regulations.label: HITECH Act
Laws and Regulations.definition: The Health Information Technology for Economic and Clinical Health Act of 2009, promoting the adoption and meaningful use of health IT.

Laws and Regulations.label: Conditions of Participation
Laws and Regulations.definition: Health and safety regulations that healthcare organizations must meet to participate in Medicare and Medicaid programs.

Laws and Regulations.label: Affordable Care Act
Laws and Regulations.definition: A comprehensive healthcare reform law enacted in 2010, also known as Obamacare.

Laws and Regulations.label: Patient Access Final Rule
Laws and Regulations.definition: A CMS rule requiring payers to build APIs to improve patient and provider access to health information.
terms: Interoperability and Patient Access final rule

Laws and Regulations.label: 42 CFR Part 2
Laws and Regulations.definition: A federal regulation protecting the confidentiality of substance use disorder patient records.

Laws and Regulations.label: No Surprises Act
Laws and Regulations.definition: A federal law that protects people covered under group and individual health plans from surprise medical bills.

Roles and Organization Types.label: Payer
Roles and Organization Types.definition: An entity, other than the patient, that finances or reimburses the cost of health services.

Roles and Organization Types.label: Beneficiary
Roles and Organization Types.definition: A person enrolled in a health insurance plan, such as Medicare or Medicaid.

Roles and Organization Types.label: Caregiver
Roles and Organization Types.definition: A person who provides direct care for children, elderly people, or people with chronic illness or disability.

Roles and Organization Types.label: Physician
Roles and Organization Types.definition: A medical doctor who has completed graduate training and is licensed to practice medicine.

Roles and Organization Types.label: Clinician
Roles and Organization Types.definition: A healthcare professional qualified in the clinical practice of medicine, such as a doctor, nurse, or therapist.

Roles and Organization Types.label: Health Plan
Roles and Organization Types.definition: An organization that provides or pays the cost of medical care, also known as an insurer.

Roles and Organization Types.label: ACO
Roles and Organization Types.definition: An Accountable Care Organization, a group of healthcare providers who agree to be accountable for the quality and cost of care.

Roles and Organization Types.label: Physician Assistant
Roles and Organization Types.definition: A licensed medical professional who holds an advanced degree and is able to provide direct patient care.

Roles and Organization Types.label: FQHC
Roles and Organization Types.definition: A Federally Qualified Health Center, a community-based healthcare provider that receives funds from HRSA to provide primary care in underserved areas.

Roles and Organization Types.label: Pharmacist
Roles and Organization Types.definition: A healthcare professional who specializes in the right way to use, store, preserve, and provide medication.

Roles and Organization Types.label: PBM
Roles and Organization Types.definition: A Pharmacy Benefit Manager, a third-party administrator of prescription drug programs for commercial health plans, self-insured employers, and government programs.

Roles and Organization Types.label: SNF
Roles and Organization Types.definition: A Skilled Nursing Facility, an inpatient healthcare facility with the staff and equipment to provide skilled nursing care.

Roles and Organization Types.label: MAC
Roles and Organization Types.definition: A Medicare Administrative Contractor, a private health care insurer that has been awarded a contract to process Medicare claims.

Roles and Organization Types.label: CBO
Roles and Organization Types.definition: A Community-Based Organization, a public or private non-profit that is representative of a community or a significant segment of a community.
